title: Abdominal aortic aneurysm - NHS
text:
risk if you:
are male and aged 65 or over smoke or used to smoke have high blood pressure have high cholesterol have a close relative who's had an abdominal aortic aneurysm have coronary or peripheral artery disease ( atherosclerosis ) have chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) have a condition such as Marfan syndrome Sometimes an abdominal aortic aneurysm can be caused by an infection, but this is rare.
Video: Abdominal aortic aneurysm 
This video shows what an abdominal aortic aneurysm looks like.
